page.content: In a study of parasites affecting pet birds (n = 63) and zoo birds (n = 83), with a total of 146 birds (n = 146), the findings indicated that for Strongyles-Capillarids, there were 0 No. positive, with a Prevalence of — and a 95% CI of — in pet birds, while zoo birds showed 13 No. positive and a Prevalence of 15.7% with a 95% CI of 7.8–23.5%, leading to a Total of 13 No. positive, a Prevalence of 8.9%, and a 95% CI of 4.3–13.6%. Ascaridia presented 10 No. positive and a Prevalence of 15.9% with a 95% CI of 6.8–24.9% in pet birds, whereas zoo birds reported 0 No. positive with a Prevalence of — and a 95% CI of —, resulting in a Total of 10 No. positive, a Prevalence of 6.8%, and a 95% CI of 2.7–10.9%. Strongyles had 1 No. positive and a Prevalence of 1.6% with a 95% CI of 0–4.7% in pet birds, while zoo birds had 7 No. positive and a Prevalence of 8.4% with a 95% CI of 2.5–14.4%, leading to a Total of 8 No. positive, a Prevalence of 5.5%, and a 95% CI of 1.8–9.2%. G. duodenalis Ass* A# exhibited 1 No. positive, a Prevalence of 4.3% with a 95% CI of 0–12.7% in pet birds, and for zoo birds, there were 3 No. positive with a Prevalence of 5.7% and a 95% CI of 0–12.1%, resulting in a Total of 4 No. positive, a Prevalence of 5.3%, and a 95% CI of 0.2–5.4%. Coccidia had 4 No. positive and a Prevalence of 6.3% with a 95% CI of 0.3–12.4% in pet birds, while zoo birds had 2 No.
